skill.name: linear-milestones-read
skill.description: List Linear project milestones from the command line using a Linear API key.
skill.summary: Read Linear project milestones through a Bun-based CLI workflow.
skill.author: Bind

security_audit.summary: Static findings for credential access, filesystem access, external commands, and network use are mostly expected for a Linear CLI skill. I found no evidence of prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration, but users must supply a Linear API key and execute a Bun command from the local skill path.

medium_findings.title: Linear API Key Required
medium_findings.description: The skill requires LINEAR_API_KEY in a .env file or exported environment variable. This is expected for Linear access, but it exposes a sensitive credential to any executed local script.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files directly require a Linear API key and describe loading it from .env or the environment. The use appears legitimate, but the credential exposure risk is real.

medium_findings.title: Local Bun Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run a Bun command from .opencode/skill/linear-milestones-read. This is normal CLI behavior, but the executed implementation is not present in the scanned file list.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command lines are explicit and require running a local script. The risk is moderated because the documented operation is read-only and no malicious command is shown.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Runtime URL Is Documentation
low_findings.description: The hardcoded bun.sh URL is a prerequisite link for installing Bun. I found no evidence that this URL receives project data or credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only in a Markdown prerequisite bullet. There is no network request or data transfer shown at this location.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Not Supported
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography finding points to the description line. I found no cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain metadata describing the skill. No evidence of weak cryptography is present in the cited file and line.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: Several static command-execution hits are Markdown option names or explanatory text. These lines do not execute commands by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are bullets and prose containing inline code formatting. They are not shell execution constructs in an executable source file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credentialed External CLI Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines a Linear API key, .env loading, a third-party SDK dependency, and local command execution. This combination is legitimate for a Linear CLI, but it requires user trust in the installed implementation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential, dependency, and command execution pieces are explicitly documented. I did not find evidence of malicious intent, so the risk is a workflow warning.

content.user_title: List Linear Milestones From the CLI
content.value_statement: Teams need quick visibility into Linear milestones without opening the full web app. This skill provides command examples for listing milestones by project, across all projects, or as JSON.
actual_capabilities: Lists milestones for a named Linear project.
Lists milestones across all Linear projects with the all option.
Limits returned milestone results with a numeric limit option.
Supports JSON output for downstream processing.
Documents required Linear API key setup.
limitations: Requires Bun to be installed before commands can run.
Requires a Linear API key in the environment or a .env file.
Only documents read operations for milestones.
Does not include the executable script in the scanned package files.

use_cases.title: Check project planning status
use_cases.description: List milestones for one Linear project before a planning meeting.

use_cases.title: Review portfolio milestones
use_cases.description: List milestones across all projects with a small result limit.

use_cases.title: Prepare milestone data for tools
use_cases.description: Request JSON output for use with command-line processors.

prompt_templates.title: List one project
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for a single project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Linear milestones skill to list milestones for the project named Mount Vernon.

prompt_templates.title: Limit all milestones
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Linear milestones skill to list milestones across all projects, limited to 10 results.

prompt_templates.title: Return JSON for one project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Linear milestones skill to list milestones for the project named Monticello with JSON output.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze selected milestones
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow after command output is available.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the Linear milestones command for my target project, then summarize overdue or upcoming milestones from the visible output.

output_examples.output: A terminal table showing milestone names, related project details, and available milestone fields from Linear.

output_examples.input: List all milestones with a limit of 10.
output_examples.output: A concise terminal result containing up to 10 milestones across Linear projects.
Structured milestone data is printed for use with tools such as filters or report generators.

best_practices: Use a Linear API key with the least access needed.
Review the installed CLI script before running it with credentials.
Use the limit option when checking many projects.
anti_patterns: Do not paste Linear API keys into prompts or shared logs.
Do not run the command in untrusted repositories.
Do not assume JSON output has been summarized unless requested.

faq.question: Does this skill modify Linear data?
faq.answer: The documented commands are read-only and focus on listing milestones.

faq.question: What credential does it need?
faq.answer: It needs LINEAR_API_KEY from Linear personal API key settings.

faq.question: Where can the API key be stored?
faq.answer: The documentation says it can be exported in the environment or placed in a .env file.

faq.question: Can it list milestones for all projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented all option lists milestones across projects.

faq.question: Can it produce machine-readable output?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented JSON option prints structured output.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The workflow uses a sensitive API key and executes a local command, even though no malicious intent was found.
